---
title: "Cell-Type-Specific Biomarkers in Human Liver Fibrosis"
subtitle: "Reproducible single-cell discovery pipeline, full report"
date: today
params:
  results_dir: "results"
  primary: "gse136103"
  datasets: "gse136103,gse244832"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
format:
  html:
    toc: true
  pdf:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage[margin=0.6in]{geometry}
        \usepackage{etoolbox}
        \AtBeginEnvironment{longtable}{\scriptsize}
        \setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
  latex:
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage[margin=0.6in]{geometry}
        \usepackage{etoolbox}
        \AtBeginEnvironment{longtable}{\scriptsize}
        \setlength{\tabcolsep}{3pt}
---

```{python}
# | tags: [parameters]
results_dir = "results"
primary = "gse136103"
datasets = "gse136103,gse244832"
```

```{python}
import json
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd

RD = Path(results_dir)
DS = [d for d in datasets.split(",") if d]


def read_tsv(p):
    p = Path(p)
    return pd.read_csv(p, sep="\t") if p.exists() else pd.DataFrame()
```

## Overview

This report is generated by the liver-fibrosis pipeline. It summarizes QC,
integration, annotation, donor-aware differential expression, pathway and cell-cell
communication mechanism analysis, and the AI/ML biomarker prioritization that yields the
ranked candidate table. Method rationale and citations are in `docs/methods.md`; written
answers to the screening questions are in `docs/written_answers.md`.

## QC summary

```{python}
qc_cols = ["sample", "n_cells_before", "n_cells_after", "pct_kept", "doublets_removed", "mito_pct_ceiling"]
for ds in DS:
    df = read_tsv(RD / f"02_qc/{ds}/qc_summary.tsv")
    if not df.empty:
        print(f"### {ds}")
        display(df[[c for c in qc_cols if c in df.columns]])
```

## Integration (scIB) and annotation

```{python}
cv_cols = ["leiden", "n_cells", "cell_type", "compartment", "frac_fibrotic_F3plus"]
for ds in DS:
    print(f"### {ds}")
    display(read_tsv(RD / f"03_integrate/{ds}/scib_metrics.tsv").round(3))
    cv = read_tsv(RD / f"04_annotate/{ds}/compartment_validation.tsv")
    display(cv[[c for c in cv_cols if c in cv.columns]].round(3) if not cv.empty else cv)
```

## Ranked biomarker / target candidates

The headline list is gated on differential-expression significance (compartment-level DESeq2
*or* a disease-associated-subcluster marker) and on cell-type specificity, so each candidate is
a genuine, compartment-specific disease signal rather than an abundant lineage marker.
Candidates flagged `in_disease_niche` are recovered from a disease-associated subpopulation that
compartment-level pseudobulk averages out (e.g. scar-associated endothelium / macrophages).

```{python}
scores = read_tsv(RD / "08_score/candidate_scores.tsv")
sel = scores[scores.get("selected", "").astype(str).str.lower() == "true"] if not scores.empty else scores
cols = [c for c in ["rank", "gene", "compartment", "composite", "log2FoldChange", "padj",
                    "accessibility_class", "druggability", "in_disease_niche"] if c in sel.columns]
if not sel.empty and "rank" in sel.columns:
    sel = sel.sort_values("rank")
display(sel[cols].round(3) if cols else scores.head(20))
```

## Known-positive recall (sanity check)

Does the pipeline recover biomarkers already established in the human-liver-fibrosis literature,
and where a known positive is missed, *why* (non-significant / filtered / absent)? Panel:
`resources/known_positive_markers.tsv`.

```{python}
display(read_tsv(RD / "08_score/known_positive_recall_summary.tsv"))
rec = read_tsv(RD / "08_score/known_positive_recall.tsv")
if not rec.empty:
    rcols = [c for c in ["gene", "compartment", "found_status", "rank", "primary_padj",
                         "validation_status", "rescued_by_validation"] if c in rec.columns]
    display(rec[rcols])
```

## Niche (subpopulation) structure

Disease-associated subclusters within each compartment (donor-aware enrichment, valid within a
sorted compartment); their one-vs-rest markers are the subset biomarkers folded into the ranked
list above, recovering signals (PLVAP, TREM2, ...) that compartment-level pseudobulk buries.

```{python}
for ds in DS:
    nsum = read_tsv(RD / f"05_de/{ds}/niche_summary.tsv")
    if not nsum.empty and "disease_associated" in nsum.columns:
        da = nsum[nsum["disease_associated"].astype(str).str.lower() == "true"]
        if not da.empty:
            print(f"### {ds}")
            display(da.sort_values("disease_log2fc", ascending=False))
```

## Cross-dataset reproducibility

```{python}
rp = read_tsv(RD / "09_crossdataset/repro_scores.tsv")
rp_cols = ["gene", "compartment", "primary_lfc", "primary_padj", "valid_lfc", "valid_padj", "repro_score"]
display(rp[[c for c in rp_cols if c in rp.columns]].head(20).round(3) if not rp.empty else rp)
```

## Mechanism: pathways and cell-cell communication

```{python}
for ds in DS:
    print(f"### {ds}")
    display(read_tsv(RD / f"06_pathway/{ds}/activity.tsv").head(15))
    display(read_tsv(RD / f"07_ccc/{ds}/differential_ccc.tsv").head(15))
```
